---
title: "Practice Exam — Two-Mean Inference"
subtitle: "Introductory Statistics · Chapters 5–11"
navbar: false
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    self-contained: true   # single shareable .html file — no server needed
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    number-sections: false
    # ── Heading styles ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    # h4  = Question number  (e.g. "Question 1")
    # h5  = Sub-part label   (e.g. "Part A")
    css: |
      h4 {
        font-size: 1rem;
        font-weight: 600;
        margin-top: 1.8em;
        margin-bottom: 0.2em;
        color: #1a1a1a;
        border-left: 3px solid #1a1a1a;
        padding-left: 0.6em;
      }
      h5 {
        font-size: 0.9rem;
        font-weight: 600;
        color: #555;
        margin-top: 1.1em;
        margin-bottom: 0.2em;
        text-transform: uppercase;
        letter-spacing: 0.06em;
      }
      /* ── Answer feedback colours ── */
      .ans-correct   { color: #2d6a2d; background: #eaf3de;
                       border-left: 3px solid #3b6d11;
                       padding: 6px 10px; border-radius: 3px;
                       margin-top: 4px; font-size: 0.88rem; }
      .ans-wrong     { color: #8b2020; background: #fcebeb;
                       border-left: 3px solid #a32d2d;
                       padding: 6px 10px; border-radius: 3px;
                       margin-top: 4px; font-size: 0.88rem; }
      .ans-pending   { color: #666; font-size: 0.85rem;
                       margin-top: 4px; font-style: italic; }
      /* ── Score box ── */
      #score-box     { background: #f4f4f0; border: 1px solid #ccc;
                       border-radius: 6px; padding: 1em 1.25em;
                       margin-top: 2em; font-size: 0.95rem; }
      /* ── Scenario / output blocks ── */
      .scenario      { border: 1.5px solid #1a1a1a; border-radius: 3px;
                       padding: 1.1em 1.3em; background: #fffef9;
                       margin: 1em 0; font-size: 0.93rem; }
      .output        { background: #f4f4f0; border-left: 3px solid #888;
                       padding: 0.75em 1em; font-family: monospace;
                       font-size: 0.84rem; white-space: pre; margin: 0.75em 0; }
      /* ── Part divider ── */
      .part-divider  { border: none; border-top: 1.5px solid #ddd;
                       margin: 2.5em 0 1em; }
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# Nothing needed here for OJS-only interactivity.
# Add library(tidyverse) etc. if you want R chunks alongside the quiz.
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
```

<!-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     HOW THIS TEMPLATE WORKS
     ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     1. Each question is a plain markdown section using #### (H4) for the
        question number and ##### (H5) for sub-parts A, B, …

     2. Each answer widget is an {ojs} code chunk.  The pattern is:

           // Define answer key (hidden from students in rendered output)
           correct_q1 = "B"

           // Radio-button widget
           viewof ans_q1 = Inputs.radio(
             ["A. option text", "B. option text", "C. option text"],
             { label: "" }
           )

           // Reactive feedback cell
           html`<div class="${fb_q1.cls}">${fb_q1.msg}</div>`

           // Feedback logic (put in same chunk or a follow-up chunk)
           fb_q1 = {
             if (ans_q1 == null)
               return { cls: "ans-pending", msg: "Select an answer above." }
             const chosen = ans_q1.split(".")[0].trim()   // extracts "A", "B", …
             return chosen === correct_q1
               ? { cls: "ans-correct", msg: "✓ Correct! " + explanation_q1 }
               : { cls: "ans-wrong",   msg: "✗ Not quite. " + explanation_q1 }
           }

     3. A live score counter at the bottom tallies all questions automatically.

     4. To ADD a new question:
        a. Copy a question block (#### … to the next ####).
        b. Rename every variable: ans_q1 → ans_q5, fb_q1 → fb_q5, etc.
        c. Add the new answer variable to the score counter ojs chunk.

     5. To HIDE the answer key from source-view, move correct_qN values into
        a separate `answers.js` file and import it:
           {ojs}  import { correct_q1, correct_q2 } from "./answers.js"
        (Only matters if students will inspect source.)
     ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ -->


---

## Instructions

This is a self-grading practice exam. Select an answer for each question — feedback appears immediately. Your running score is shown at the bottom of the page.

---

## Part 1 — Models & Variability

<hr class="part-divider">

#### Question 1

A researcher uses an empty model to predict student exam scores. The sample has $n = 40$, $\bar{y} = 72$, and $\text{SST} = 3600$.

##### Part A

What is the predicted value $\hat{y}_i$ for every student under the empty model?

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

// ── Answer key ──────────────────────────────────────────────
correct_q1a = "B"
explanation_q1a = "The empty model has no predictors and predicts the sample mean ȳ = 72 for every observation. It has exactly 1 parameter: β₀ = ȳ."

// ── Widget ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
viewof ans_q1a = Inputs.radio(
  [
    "A. 0",
    "B. 72 (the sample mean)",
    "C. The student's individual score",
    "D. The median score"
  ],
  { label: "" }
)

// ── Feedback ────────────────────────────────────────────────
fb_q1a = {
  if (ans_q1a == null) return { cls: "ans-pending", msg: "Select an answer above." }
  const chosen = ans_q1a.split(".")[0].trim()
  return chosen === correct_q1a
    ? { cls: "ans-correct", msg: "✓ Correct!  " + explanation_q1a }
    : { cls: "ans-wrong",   msg: "✗ Not quite.  " + explanation_q1a }
}

html`<div class="${fb_q1a.cls}">${fb_q1a.msg}</div>`
```

##### Part B

What is $R^2$ if the model SSE = 1800?

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

correct_q1b = "B"
explanation_q1b = "R² = SSM/SST = (SST − SSE)/SST = (3600 − 1800)/3600 = 1800/3600 = 0.50. The model explains 50% of the variability."

viewof ans_q1b = Inputs.radio(
  [
    "A. 0.25",
    "B. 0.50",
    "C. 0.75",
    "D. 2.00"
  ],
  { label: "" }
)

fb_q1b = {
  if (ans_q1b == null) return { cls: "ans-pending", msg: "Select an answer above." }
  const chosen = ans_q1b.split(".")[0].trim()
  return chosen === correct_q1b
    ? { cls: "ans-correct", msg: "✓ Correct!  " + explanation_q1b }
    : { cls: "ans-wrong",   msg: "✗ Not quite.  " + explanation_q1b }
}

html`<div class="${fb_q1b.cls}">${fb_q1b.msg}</div>`
```

---

#### Question 2

Which of the following correctly classifies a model that includes one numerical predictor and one binary categorical predictor?

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

correct_q2 = "C"
explanation_q2 = "This model has β₀ (intercept) + β₁ (numerical) + β₂ (binary categorical) = 3 parameters, making it a complex model."

viewof ans_q2 = Inputs.radio(
  [
    "A. Empty model — 1 parameter",
    "B. Simple model — 2 parameters",
    "C. Complex model — 3 or more parameters",
    "D. Cannot be classified without knowing n"
  ],
  { label: "" }
)

fb_q2 = {
  if (ans_q2 == null) return { cls: "ans-pending", msg: "Select an answer above." }
  const chosen = ans_q2.split(".")[0].trim()
  return chosen === correct_q2
    ? { cls: "ans-correct", msg: "✓ Correct!  " + explanation_q2 }
    : { cls: "ans-wrong",   msg: "✗ Not quite.  " + explanation_q2 }
}

html`<div class="${fb_q2.cls}">${fb_q2.msg}</div>`
```

---

## Part 2 — Hypothesis Testing

<hr class="part-divider">

#### Question 3

A p-value of 0.03 is obtained with $\alpha = 0.05$.

##### Part A

What is the correct decision?

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

correct_q3a = "B"
explanation_q3a = "Since p = 0.03 < α = 0.05, we reject H₀. There is statistically significant evidence in favor of the alternative hypothesis."

viewof ans_q3a = Inputs.radio(
  [
    "A. Fail to reject H₀ — not enough evidence",
    "B. Reject H₀ — evidence supports Hₐ",
    "C. Accept H₀ — the null is true",
    "D. The result is inconclusive"
  ],
  { label: "" }
)

fb_q3a = {
  if (ans_q3a == null) return { cls: "ans-pending", msg: "Select an answer above." }
  const chosen = ans_q3a.split(".")[0].trim()
  return chosen === correct_q3a
    ? { cls: "ans-correct", msg: "✓ Correct!  " + explanation_q3a }
    : { cls: "ans-wrong",   msg: "✗ Not quite.  " + explanation_q3a }
}

html`<div class="${fb_q3a.cls}">${fb_q3a.msg}</div>`
```

##### Part B

Which type of error could you be making with this decision?

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

correct_q3b = "A"
explanation_q3b = "When we reject H₀, the error we might be making is a Type I error — rejecting H₀ when it is actually true. P(Type I error) = α = 0.05."

viewof ans_q3b = Inputs.radio(
  [
    "A. Type I error — rejecting a true H₀",
    "B. Type II error — failing to reject a false H₀",
    "C. Both Type I and Type II simultaneously",
    "D. Neither — the decision is guaranteed correct"
  ],
  { label: "" }
)

fb_q3b = {
  if (ans_q3b == null) return { cls: "ans-pending", msg: "Select an answer above." }
  const chosen = ans_q3b.split(".")[0].trim()
  return chosen === correct_q3b
    ? { cls: "ans-correct", msg: "✓ Correct!  " + explanation_q3b }
    : { cls: "ans-wrong",   msg: "✗ Not quite.  " + explanation_q3b }
}

html`<div class="${fb_q3b.cls}">${fb_q3b.msg}</div>`
```

---

#### Question 4

You run 5 separate t-tests each at $\alpha = 0.05$ to compare group means. What is the primary concern?

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

correct_q4 = "C"
explanation_q4 = "Running multiple tests inflates the family-wise Type I error rate. With 5 tests at α = 0.05, P(at least one false positive) ≈ 1 − 0.95⁵ ≈ 0.23. Use ANOVA first, then correct α for follow-up tests."

viewof ans_q4 = Inputs.radio(
  [
    "A. Each individual test becomes more powerful",
    "B. The Type II error rate increases dramatically",
    "C. The overall Type I error rate inflates above 0.05",
    "D. The t-distribution is no longer valid"
  ],
  { label: "" }
)

fb_q4 = {
  if (ans_q4 == null) return { cls: "ans-pending", msg: "Select an answer above." }
  const chosen = ans_q4.split(".")[0].trim()
  return chosen === correct_q4
    ? { cls: "ans-correct", msg: "✓ Correct!  " + explanation_q4 }
    : { cls: "ans-wrong",   msg: "✗ Not quite.  " + explanation_q4 }
}

html`<div class="${fb_q4.cls}">${fb_q4.msg}</div>`
```

---

## Part 3 — Two-Mean Inference

<hr class="part-divider">

The following scenario is used for Questions 5–7.

::: scenario
Researchers compared resting heart rate (bpm) between two groups. Summary statistics:

| Group | n | ȳ | s |
|---|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| Exercise | 30 | 62.4 | 8.1 |
| Sedentary | 35 | 71.8 | 9.4 |

R output:

```
Welch Two Sample t-test
t = -4.47,  df = 61.3,  p-value < 0.001
95% CI:  (-13.5,  -5.3)
```
:::

#### Question 5

##### Part A

What are the null and alternative hypotheses?

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

correct_q5a = "B"
explanation_q5a = "H₀: μ_Exercise = μ_Sedentary (no difference), Hₐ: μ_Exercise ≠ μ_Sedentary (a difference exists). This is a two-sided test."

viewof ans_q5a = Inputs.radio(
  [
    "A. H₀: μ_E > μ_S  vs.  Hₐ: μ_E < μ_S",
    "B. H₀: μ_E = μ_S  vs.  Hₐ: μ_E ≠ μ_S",
    "C. H₀: μ_E ≠ μ_S  vs.  Hₐ: μ_E = μ_S",
    "D. H₀: ȳ_E = ȳ_S  vs.  Hₐ: ȳ_E ≠ ȳ_S"
  ],
  { label: "" }
)

fb_q5a = {
  if (ans_q5a == null) return { cls: "ans-pending", msg: "Select an answer above." }
  const chosen = ans_q5a.split(".")[0].trim()
  return chosen === correct_q5a
    ? { cls: "ans-correct", msg: "✓ Correct!  " + explanation_q5a }
    : { cls: "ans-wrong",   msg: "✗ Not quite.  " + explanation_q5a }
}

html`<div class="${fb_q5a.cls}">${fb_q5a.msg}</div>`
```

##### Part B

What is the observed difference in sample means (Exercise − Sedentary)?

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

correct_q5b = "A"
explanation_q5b = "ȳ_Exercise − ȳ_Sedentary = 62.4 − 71.8 = −9.4 bpm. The negative sign indicates exercise group has lower heart rate."

viewof ans_q5b = Inputs.radio(
  [
    "A. −9.4 bpm",
    "B. +9.4 bpm",
    "C. −4.47 bpm",
    "D. 134.2 bpm"
  ],
  { label: "" }
)

fb_q5b = {
  if (ans_q5b == null) return { cls: "ans-pending", msg: "Select an answer above." }
  const chosen = ans_q5b.split(".")[0].trim()
  return chosen === correct_q5b
    ? { cls: "ans-correct", msg: "✓ Correct!  " + explanation_q5b }
    : { cls: "ans-wrong",   msg: "✗ Not quite.  " + explanation_q5b }
}

html`<div class="${fb_q5b.cls}">${fb_q5b.msg}</div>`
```

---

#### Question 6

Interpret the 95% confidence interval (−13.5, −5.3) in context.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

correct_q6 = "B"
explanation_q6 = "The CI gives a plausible range for the TRUE difference μ_E − μ_S. Because both bounds are negative, we have evidence the exercise group's mean heart rate is lower. We are 95% confident the true difference is between −13.5 and −5.3 bpm."

viewof ans_q6 = Inputs.radio(
  [
    "A. 95% of individual heart rates fall between −13.5 and −5.3 bpm",
    "B. We are 95% confident the true difference in mean heart rates (Exercise − Sedentary) is between −13.5 and −5.3 bpm",
    "C. There is a 95% probability that H₀ is false",
    "D. The exercise group will always have a heart rate 5.3 to 13.5 bpm lower than sedentary"
  ],
  { label: "" }
)

fb_q6 = {
  if (ans_q6 == null) return { cls: "ans-pending", msg: "Select an answer above." }
  const chosen = ans_q6.split(".")[0].trim()
  return chosen === correct_q6
    ? { cls: "ans-correct", msg: "✓ Correct!  " + explanation_q6 }
    : { cls: "ans-wrong",   msg: "✗ Not quite.  " + explanation_q6 }
}

html`<div class="${fb_q6.cls}">${fb_q6.msg}</div>`
```

---

#### Question 7

What assumption is required for the Welch t-test to be valid here?

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

correct_q7 = "C"
explanation_q7 = "The key assumptions are (1) independence of observations within and between groups, (2) normality of errors OR large n. Note: Welch's t-test does NOT require equal variances — that's the pooled t-test."

viewof ans_q7 = Inputs.radio(
  [
    "A. The two groups must have equal sample sizes",
    "B. The two groups must have equal variances (σ_E = σ_S)",
    "C. Observations are independent AND errors are approximately normal (or n is large)",
    "D. The data must come from a randomized experiment"
  ],
  { label: "" }
)

fb_q7 = {
  if (ans_q7 == null) return { cls: "ans-pending", msg: "Select an answer above." }
  const chosen = ans_q7.split(".")[0].trim()
  return chosen === correct_q7
    ? { cls: "ans-correct", msg: "✓ Correct!  " + explanation_q7 }
    : { cls: "ans-wrong",   msg: "✗ Not quite.  " + explanation_q7 }
}

html`<div class="${fb_q7.cls}">${fb_q7.msg}</div>`
```

---

## Score

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

// ── Tally all parts ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// Add every answer variable here when you add new questions.
// Each entry: 1 if correct, 0 if wrong, null if not yet answered.

answered_list = [ans_q1a, ans_q1b, ans_q2, ans_q3a, ans_q3b,
                 ans_q4,  ans_q5a, ans_q5b, ans_q6,  ans_q7]

correct_list  = ["B", "B", "C", "B", "A",
                 "C", "B", "A", "B", "C"]

total    = answered_list.length
answered = answered_list.filter(a => a != null).length

correct_count = answered_list.reduce((sum, a, i) => {
  if (a == null) return sum
  return a.split(".")[0].trim() === correct_list[i] ? sum + 1 : sum
}, 0)

pct = total > 0 ? Math.round((correct_count / total) * 100) : 0

html`
<div id="score-box">
  <strong>Score: ${correct_count} / ${total} &nbsp;(${pct}%)</strong><br>
  <span style="font-size:0.85rem;color:#555">
    ${answered} of ${total} questions answered
    ${answered < total ? " — keep going!" : " — all done!"}
  </span>
  <div style="margin-top:10px;height:8px;background:#ddd;border-radius:4px;overflow:hidden">
    <div style="width:${pct}%;height:100%;background:${pct>=80?'#3B6D11':pct>=60?'#BA7517':'#A32D2D'};border-radius:4px;transition:width 0.4s"></div>
  </div>
</div>
`
```
